projects.title: Artist Residency : Elvira.
projects.author: Elvira
projects.shortdescription: This residency broke down conventional boundaries between craft and art.
projects.artistdescription: Multi-media artist. Collaborative process is inherent to the work.
projects.details: Collaborative Engagement. Sharing artistic techniques as a way to build a sense of community. This residency broke down conventional boundaries between craft and art. Integrating and examining traditional categories of “artisanal” and “artistic” is a key element.

projects.title: Mapping Workshop
projects.author: Margie Ruddrick, Vritti, Hibah
projects.shortdescription: The Shillim Institute Mapping Workshop explored the landscape through art, ecology, and culture to deepen connections between people and place.
projects.artistdescription: Led by Margie Ruddrick, Vritti, and Hibah, this collaborative workshop brought together diverse perspectives to explore the Shillim landscape through multiple lenses.
projects.details: The Shillim Institute Mapping Workshop engaged participants in exploring the Shillim landscape through artistic, ecological, and cultural lenses. It fostered collective observation, documentation, and interpretation of natural and human ecosystems, creating visual and experiential maps that deepen understanding of place, memory, and sustainable coexistence within the Sahyadri environment.

projects.title: Conservation Fellowship
projects.author: Adhith M K
projects.shortdescription: Analysing the Bird Species Distribution Using Regional Custom CNN Model for Evaluating Restoration Success in the Northern Western Ghats Landscape
projects.artistdescription: Adhith is a post graduate in Ecological Informatics from Kerala University of Digital Science, Innovation and Technology. His research integrates wildlife ecology with advanced computational approaches, focusing on acoustic monitoring, species behaviour, and conservation technology. He has conducted extensive fieldwork across diverse ecosystems and has experience in biodiversity assessment, remote sensing, GIS, Python, R, and deep learning.
projects.details: Adhith is a post graduate in Ecological Informatics from Kerala University of Digital Science, Innovation and Technology. His research integrates wildlife ecology with advanced computational approaches, focusing on acoustic monitoring, species behaviour, and conservation technology. He has conducted extensive fieldwork across diverse ecosystems and has experience in biodiversity assessment, remote sensing, GIS, Python, R, and deep learning. At Shillim Institute, he has led research on avian acoustic ecology in the Northern Western Ghats, developing data-driven tools to evaluate habitat restoration. He has a broader interests in wildlife conservation, ecosystem restoration, and sustainable development, with a commitment to advancing science-based conservation solutions

projects.title: Conservation Fellowship
projects.author: Dr. Sangavi Dhanapal
projects.shortdescription: Diversity and Distribution of Spiders in Sahyadri hills: A Natural and Altered habitat in Shillim Institute, Pune, Western Ghats
projects.artistdescription: Dr. Sangavi Dhanapal is a biodiversity researcher specializing in spider ecology, taxonomy, and conservation biology. Her studies have documented over 180 spider species from Salem District, Tamil Nadu, the first comprehensive record for the region. She previously worked at the National Institute of Advanced Studies under Prof. Anindya Sinha, studying spider diversity in Assam, and gained international experience through research internships at the National Pingtung University of Science and Technology, Taiwan. Dr. Sangavi has authored four peer-reviewed papers, two book chapters, and received awards for her research presentations.
projects.details: A detailed study of spider diversity was carried out across six different habitats of the Shillim landscape in the northern Western Ghats. Over the course of the study, nearly 500 individuals were recorded, representing 200 species across 25 families- an impressive 10% of India’s known spider diversity. The findings highlight Shillim’s ecological richness, with natural habitats like shrublands, forests, and grasslands supporting the highest variety of species. Among these, the jumping spiders (Salticidae) emerged as the most dominant family, followed by orb weavers (Araneidae) and lynx spiders (Oxyopidae). Several species were documented for the first time in Maharashtra, and 26 species recorded are endemic to India. Spiders play an important ecological role as natural pest controllers and as indicators of habitat health. The study’s results suggest that healthy, undisturbed ecosystems at Shillim provide essential niches for these invertebrates, further reinforcing the success of ongoing habitat restoration work. This fellowship builds a strong foundation for tracking invertebrate diversity and deepening our understanding of how restored ecosystems sustain complex web-of-life interactions.

projects.title: Fire Lines
projects.author: Green Learning Institute
projects.shortdescription: Annual project : Creating firelines in Shillim and Pavna Catchment area for a safe boundary for thriving biodiversity
projects.artistdescription: Green Learning Institute is dedicated to environmental education and conservation practices, working to create sustainable solutions for forest and grassland management in collaboration with local communities.
projects.details: Fire lines (also known as firebreaks or fire corridors) are crucial tools in forest and grassland management for preventing, controlling, and managing wildfires. They are deliberately cleared strips of land—free of vegetation and other flammable materials—designed to stop or slow the spread of fire.

projects.title: Artist Residency : Sanju Jain
projects.author: Sanju Jain
projects.shortdescription: Multi Media artist working with natural pigments.
projects.artistdescription: Sanju Jain is a multi-media artist who skillfully combines art and science while upholding conservation and sustainable practices by utilizing natural pigments. Her artwork is a stunning representation of landscapes, captivating audiences through various prestigious exhibitions across the nation.
projects.details: Conservation, sustainable art practices and education:  A quote from her: “I found myself for the first time in Shillim “She skillfully combined art and science while upholding the Shillim Ethos of conservation and sustainable practices by utilizing natural pigments. Her workshop with rural children provided a fresh perspective for the new generation, who will bear the responsibility of conservation in the future. Her artwork is a stunning representation of the SHILLIM landscape, captivating everyone who witnesses her creativity. Additionally, she has shown her journey at Shillim through her art pieces in various prestigious exhibitions across the nation.

projects.title: Artist Residency : Shatabdi Mallik
projects.author: Shatabdi Mallik
projects.shortdescription: Odissi dancer working in ancient Indian traditions
projects.artistdescription: Shatabdi Mallik is an internationally recognized Odissi dance artist who integrates classical Indian culture with contemporary practice. Her commitment to educating students through interdisciplinary dance forms has fostered positive engagement with cultural institutions and brought visibility to traditional art forms.
projects.details: Cultural conservation, education, and organizational engagement – An internationally recognized Odissi dance artist, who integrates classical culture with the local landscape. Her initiative to teach ten rural girls and perform in a concert has increased the visibility of the Shillim Institute to a broader audience, and has also introduced Shillim to the Ministry of Culture and Infosys, which will ultimately aid in fundraising efforts. Her commitment to educating master's students at Lalit Kala Kendra through interdisciplinary dance forms has fostered positive organizational engagement, further solidifying Shillim's reputation through newspaper coverage and various reputable organizations.

projects.title: Artist Residency : Constantine and Rebecca
projects.author: Constantine and Rebecca
projects.shortdescription: Dancers/choreographer in European/US modern dance tradition.
projects.artistdescription: Constantine and Rebecca are dancers and choreographers working in the European/US modern dance tradition, exploring the relationship between movement, landscape, and site-specific performance.
projects.details: Forms and Movement in the Landscape.  Dance etudes explore ways to interact with the landscape as a site of performance and a source of inspiration.

projects.title: Artist Residency : Jignesh Sheth
projects.author: Jignesh Sheth
projects.shortdescription: Master performer on table; composer and performer working in “fusion” idiom.
projects.artistdescription: Jignesh Sheth is a master tabla performer holding a master's degree in the instrument. He specializes in fusion music, collaborating with various artists including local talents, and bridging classical Indian traditions with contemporary musical forms.
projects.details: Collaborative engagement - A transformative journey at Shillim. A musician holding a master's degree in Tabla (the instrument). His collaboration with various artists, including local talents, exemplifies this. The fusion of instruments ranging from Classical to local traditional Dholki and Pakhwaj served as a progressive approach to cultural conservation and promotion, captivating the diverse audience in Pune bringing Visibility of Shillim to larger audience. He composed a musical piece that beautifully integrates the artist's experiences within the Shillim landscape, highlighting the connection between music and the environment.

projects.title: Artist Residency : Tanya Marcuse
projects.author: Tanya Marcuse
projects.shortdescription: Photographer, works often with found objects in the natural world.
projects.artistdescription: Tanya Marcuse is a photographer who works with found objects in the natural world, using innovative lighting techniques to reveal and transform her subjects. Her work explores the intersection of nature, light, and space.
projects.details: Revelation and Transformation: A quote from Tanya’s presentation of “Sacred Grove” indicates how a photographer works in a landscape inspired a new understanding of lighting.  “I used lighting to both reveal and transform that closer space, working to describe the lyrical gestures of vines, layers of shallow depth, and even, at times, lighting the thickness of the air. An orb of light opens up a portal that can, perhaps, work as both positive and negative space, both an absence and a presence.”

projects.title: Artist Residency : Ann De Forest
projects.author: Ann De Forest
projects.shortdescription: Walking artist/writer; connects walking practices with community organizing
projects.artistdescription: Ann De Forest is a walking artist and writer who connects walking practices with community organizing. Her work explores the intersections of movement, place, and environmental awareness through experiential and collaborative approaches.
projects.details: Walking as a Guest: From Ignorance to Intimacy.  Ann produced an illustrated text that describes the author’s experience of immersion in the Shillim environment. The text is very quotable throughout and speaks eloquently about the experience of being a guest in the natural world.

projects.title: Artist Residency : Kushala Vohra
projects.author: Kushala Vohra
projects.shortdescription: Photography, video, sculpture, mixed media
projects.artistdescription: Kushala Vohra is a multi-disciplinary artist working across photography, video, sculpture, and mixed media. Her practice explores the relational nature of knowledge and aesthetic experience through diverse artistic approaches.
projects.details: Mama’s Lens; Learning in Relation:  In her PowerPoint through images and videos, she documents the relational nature of knowledge and aesthetic experience in the landscape.

projects.title: Artist Residency : Joel Gordan
projects.author: Joel Gordan
projects.shortdescription: Master sound engineer.
projects.artistdescription: Joel Gordan is a master sound engineer with expertise in bioacoustic recording and conservation. His technical mastery in representing sound in space and clarifying sound envelopes across frequency bands creates robust scientific data.
projects.details: Bioacoustic Conservation: What stands out is the way that traditional techniques related to “arts” recording—representing sound in space and clarifying the envelopes of sounds in different frequency bands—creates a robust kind of scientific data. Additionally, this sound library can be used as audio in some of the videos we produce.

projects.title: Artist Residency : Erin Gee
projects.author: Erin Gee
projects.shortdescription: Composer, “contemporary classical,” European and American artistic network
projects.artistdescription: Erin Gee is a contemporary classical composer working within European and American artistic networks. Her practice explores the intersection of vocal sound production, bioacoustic feedback, and environmental soundscapes.
projects.details: Bioacoustic feedback: A quote from Erin’s “Shillim Quartet Program Note” shows how a contemporary composer’s practice was transformed by the Shillim experience: “Aaron Mendonca, who had recently graduated from the Harvard School of Design and was a fellow member of the One Landscape Collective, spoke to me very eloquently about how ‘sounds can activate biodynamic energy,’ including the possibilities for sounds to stimulate regrowth within a damaged old-growth forest. I realize now that the biofeedback process that Aaron described happened to me. The sounds of the ravine activated a dynamic change in my understanding of what vocal sound production is, and so it has ruffled the way I think about my work and creative life going forward.”

projects.title: Green Squad : Know Your Local Biodiversity
projects.author: Shillim Institute
projects.shortdescription: Module about the local biodiversity
projects.artistdescription: Developed by the Shillim Institute in collaboration with ATREE and other experts.
projects.details:
The Green Squad program helps students understand their local environment and the challenges affecting it. By working with school students in the Pavana catchment area of Maval, the program encourages them to observe nature around their villages and schools. This helps students develop a sense of responsibility towards their surroundings and builds awareness about conservation from an early age.T

This Module aimed to help students understand local biodiversity and its significance, identify native and non-native plant species and their roles in the ecosystem.

In the long term, the Green Squad program is helping build environmentally aware youth who understand local issues and can contribute to conservation efforts. By encouraging curiosity, observation, and responsibility, the program supports sustainable environmental practices in the Pavana catchment area.

projects.title: Green Squad: Exploring Sources of Water and the Water Cycle
projects.author: Shilim Institute
projects.shortdescription: Module about the rainfall , water and pollution
projects.artistdescription: Green Squad is an environmental education program by the Shillim Institute (SI), designed to teach students about the environment and biodiversity through a series of 10 curated modules. The program is tailored for students from Standards 5th to 7th and is implemented across Zilla Parishad (ZP) schools in rural Pune. Each module is conducted over the span of one month, focusing on a specific environmental theme to foster awareness, curiosity, and action among young learners.
projects.details: This module aims to change students' mindsets about the water crisis in their region, raising awareness about local water sources and their responsible usage. Exploring the theme of water pollution will highlight the significant human impact on water bodies and its adverse effects on both human health and wildlife.

projects.title: Gully Plugs & Stone Lines
projects.author: Shillim Institute
projects.shortdescription: Community project for stopping soil loss
projects.details: Gully plugs and stone lines are well-known soil and water conservation techniques, especially used in areas facing erosion, water runoff, and land degradation. They are particularly effective in hilly, semi-arid, and degraded landscapes.

projects.title: "ABHIVYAKTI " A Skill Development Program for Rural Kids
projects.author: Shillim Institute
projects.shortdescription: A skill development program for rural kids through performing arts to promote conservation.
projects.details: Abhivyakti was launched as a pilot project to revive and restore theregion’s fading art and cultural heritage. This performing arts-based skill development initiative aims to enrich rural lives by nurturing creativity, preserving cultural traditions, and empowering  aims to enrich rural lives by nurturing creativity, preserving cultural traditions, and empowering youth with artistic and entrepreneurial skills. Through inclusive training in dance, music, theatre,and instrumental arts, Abhivyakti fosters self-expression and community engagement, making art a powerful tool for social and economic transformation.

projects.title: Regenerative Farming
projects.author: Shillim Insitute
projects.shortdescription: From the Ghats to Growth: Farming for the Future
projects.details: A comprehensive regenerative farming and sustainability program that blends ecological restoration with community empowerment, nestled in the Western Ghats near Pune and Mumbai

projects.title: Green Squad :  Material Donation
projects.author: Shillim Institute/Green Squad
projects.shortdescription: The Shillim Institute donated school stationery materials and essential equipment to support local schools.
projects.details: The Shillim Institute donated school stationery materials and essential equipment to support local schools. The donation included CCTV cameras, cupboards, shoe racks, and plate-holder shelves for school use.
